hooks.command: cmd="$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty')" && case "$cmd" in *"terraform destroy"*|*"terraform apply -destroy"*) for env in production main master prod develop; do if echo "$cmd" | grep -E "environment=$env\\b|TF_VAR_environment=$env\\b|/$env/" >/dev/null; then echo '{"decision":"block","reason":"Refusing terraform destroy against protected env: '$env'. The skeleton mitigates this (pitfall #13) — destroy denylist is production|main|master|prod|develop. If you need to wipe a non-protected env, name it explicitly."}'; exit 0; fi; done ;; esac && case "$cmd" in *"aws s3 rb"*|*"aws s3api delete-bucket"*) if echo "$cmd" | grep -E "frontend-production|terraform-state" >/dev/null; then echo '{"decision":"block","reason":"Refusing to delete production frontend or terraform state bucket. These are singletons in aws-setup/."}'; exit 0; fi ;; esac && exit 0

path="$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty')" && case "$path" in */aws-setup/*|*/.ralph/PROMPT.md|*/.ralph/specs/*|*/.ralphrc|*/scripts/derive-env-name.sh) echo '{"permissionDecision":"ask","permissionDecisionReason":"Editing protected substrate file ('$path'). The skeleton or .ralph/ files are NOT meant to be modified casually. Proceed only if this iteration explicitly requires it."}'; exit 0 ;; esac; exit 0
path="$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty')" && case "$path" in *.go) gofmt -w "$path" 2>/dev/null && echo '{"systemMessage":"gofmt applied to '$path'"}' ;; esac; exit 0
